What are you looking for?

Four Ways to Transact,
One Standard of Trust

Whether you're renting a studio in Lagos or buying land in Abuja — every transaction goes through the same verified, escrow-backed process.

🏠

Rent

Monthly to yearly residential rentals. Verified landlord, signed agreement, escrow deposit.

📍 Nationwide 🔒 Escrow
📋

Lease

2–10 year commercial or residential leases. Lawyer-drafted agreements. Staged payments.

⚖️ Lawyer 📄 Agreement
🔑

Buy

Full property purchase. Title verified, C of O confirmed, Deed of Assignment on-platform.

📜 C of O 🛡️ Verified
💰

Sell

List your verified property. Reach qualified, identity-checked buyers nationwide.

10% on close
The Process

From Listing to Keys —
Every Step Protected

1
🪪

Verify Your Identity

Submit your NIN, bio data, and a government ID. Our team cross-checks against NIMC records. No fake names. No ghost landlords.

2
📄

Submit Property Docs

Upload your C of O, Survey Plan, or Deed of Assignment. Our lawyers verify against state land registry records before approval.

3
🤝

Transact on Platform

Both parties sign a digital agreement. Payments are made via bank transfer to GeoEstate's designated account — our dedicated escrow account is currently being processed and will be activated in Phase 2. Zero off-platform payments. Zero illegal fees. The consent agreement prohibits it.

4
🔓

Handover & Release

Confirm handover on the platform. GeoEstate transfers 90% to the property owner (less any applicable buyer service fee). Both parties receive a signed receipt and document vault. Full escrow automation activates in Phase 2.

Owner Portal

List Your Property

Your listing will only go live after we verify your identity and property documents. This protects you and every buyer on the platform.

1
2
3
4
Your IdentityProperty DetailsDocumentsConsent

🪪 Step 1 — Your Identity

✅ Our team manually verifies your documents within 24–72 hours. No automated API needed — a real reviewer checks every submission.

🏠 Step 2 — Property Details

📸
Click to upload property photos
Images upload directly to Cloudinary · Min. 4 photos

📄 Step 3 — Title Documents

Upload the applicable title documents. Our lawyers verify these against state land registry records before your listing is approved.

Certificate of Occupancy (C of O)
Required for most residential and commercial properties
Survey Plan
Boundary and land area confirmation
Deed of Assignment / Governor's Consent
Required if property was transferred from a previous owner
Excision / Gazette (if applicable)
For community or excised land
⏱ Verification SLA: 3–5 business days
Our team will review your documents and contact you if anything is missing. Your listing will only go live after approval.

✍️ Step 4 — Consent Agreement

Read and sign the GeoEstate Platform Consent Agreement. This is legally binding.

GEOESTATE PLATFORM CONSENT AGREEMENT — PROPERTY OWNER

By signing this agreement, I ("the Owner") confirm and agree to the following:

1. Identity Verification: I consent to GeoEstate's internal verification team manually reviewing my submitted identity documents (government-issued photo ID and live selfie) and bio data. I confirm that all submitted information is accurate and truthful. GeoEstate is solely responsible for the verification process and the security of submitted documents.

2. Property Ownership: I confirm that I am the lawful owner of the property listed, or have the legal authority to list it on behalf of the owner. I confirm that the title documents submitted are genuine and unencumbered. GeoEstate will verify these documents against state land registry records before approving any listing.

3. Platform Fee: I agree to GeoEstate's 10% platform service fee, deducted from the transaction amount upon successful deal close. This fee covers escrow management, identity verification, legal review, and platform services. The fee is non-negotiable and non-refundable once a transaction is completed.

4. No Off-Platform Payments: I agree NOT to request, accept, or facilitate any payment outside the GeoEstate escrow system. Any off-platform payment request constitutes a breach of this agreement and may result in account suspension and referral to the Nigeria Police Force CID. During Phase 1, all payments must be made by bank transfer to the GeoEstate designated account — details will be provided by the GeoEstate sales team.

5. Law Enforcement Cooperation: I consent to GeoEstate sharing my verified identity and transaction records with the Nigeria Police Force (NPF) Criminal Investigation Department (CID) in the event of a fraud complaint or dispute. GeoEstate maintains a formal MOU with the NPF CID for this purpose.

6. Data Protection: I consent to GeoEstate storing my verification status and property records in compliance with the Nigeria Data Protection Act 2023 (NDPA). Raw NIN data will not be stored. I may request deletion of my data at any time by contacting GeoEstate.

7. GeoEstate Responsibilities: GeoEstate is responsible for: (a) securing all submitted documents; (b) conducting property title verification; (c) managing escrow funds in a CBN-compliant account; (d) providing legal review through NBA-registered lawyers; (e) coordinating with the NPF CID on fraud cases.

8. Accuracy of Listing: I confirm that all property information, photos, and pricing submitted are accurate. Misleading listings will result in immediate removal and may result in legal action under Nigerian law.
🎉

Submission Received!

Your property listing is under review. Our team will verify your identity and documents within 3–5 business days. You'll receive an email update.

The Platform

How Popson Works

A verified, escrow-backed, lawyer-reviewed, police-linked real estate platform. Here's every layer of protection we've built.

🪪

Layer 1 — Identity Verification (Both Parties)

Every property owner and every renter/buyer must verify their identity before any transaction begins. Verification is a 3-step manual process: upload a government-issued photo ID (National ID, Driver's Licence, Voter's Card, or International Passport), submit a live selfie for face comparison, and confirm your phone number via OTP. A next-of-kin declaration adds a third layer of accountability. Our internal team reviews every submission within 24 hours. NIMC API integration is planned for a future phase and will add automated verification without changing anything users see.

📄

Layer 2 — Property Title Verification

Before a listing goes live, our internal team — supported by a panel of NBA-registered lawyers — verifies the property's title documents against state land registry records. We check for encumbrances, government acquisition zones, ownership disputes, and document authenticity. Only properties that pass this review appear on the platform.

⚖️

Layer 3 — Lawyer Panel

All buy transactions and commercial leases include a review by a Popson-panel lawyer registered with the Nigerian Bar Association (NBA). The lawyer reviews title documents, drafts or reviews the transaction agreement, and certifies the deal before funds are released from escrow. Legal fees are charged separately from the platform fee.

🔒

Layer 4 — Escrow-Protected Payments

Phase 1 (Current): All payments are made by bank transfer to GeoEstate's designated account. Our dedicated CBN-compliant escrow account is currently being processed and will be integrated in Phase 2. Funds are held by GeoEstate and released only when both parties confirm handover on the platform. 90% goes to the property owner; 10% is GeoEstate's platform fee (plus a 5% service fee from the buyer/renter). If a listing is found fraudulent after payment, the full amount is refunded within 5 business days.

Phase 2 (Coming Soon): Full Paystack-powered escrow — automated, ring-fenced, and CBN-compliant.

👮

Layer 5 — Nigeria Police Force CID Liaison

Popson Geospatial Services maintains a formal MOU with the Nigeria Police Force Criminal Investigation Department (CID). When a fraud complaint is raised, the platform automatically generates a case file containing the verified identities of both parties, the property records, and the full transaction trail — and submits it to the NPF liaison. Victims no longer need to "prove" fraud from scratch.

📍

Layer 6 — Geospatial Intelligence

Every property is pinned to a precise GPS coordinate. Listing photos must be geo-tagged and taken at the property location — we validate EXIF coordinates against the declared address (within 200m). The interactive map lets you search by state, LGA, or draw a custom area. Future layers will add proximity intelligence: schools, hospitals, police stations, and infrastructure.

Ready to transact safely?

⚠️ Dispute & Fraud Reporting

File a Complaint

If you have been defrauded, extorted, or encountered an illegal fee demand — report it here. Every complaint is reviewed by our team within 2 hours and escalated to the Nigeria Police Force CID where required. Your verified identity protects you.

🛡️
Your report is protected. All dispute reports are handled confidentially. Your identity is shared with law enforcement only when a formal NPF referral is made — and only with your awareness. Off-platform fee demands are a criminal offence under Nigerian law.

Legal

Privacy Policy

Effective date: 7 June 2026 · Popson Geospatial Services · In compliance with the Nigeria Data Protection Act 2023 (NDPA)

1. Who We Are

GeoEstate is a geospatial real estate marketplace operated by Popson Geospatial Services. Our platform connects verified property owners with verified renters and buyers across Nigeria. We are committed to protecting your personal data in compliance with the Nigeria Data Protection Act 2023 (NDPA) and the Nigeria Data Protection Regulation (NDPR).

Contact: Popoola Idris Bamigboye · popson0205@gmail.com · +234 916 042 0100

2. What Data We Collect

We collect the following categories of personal data when you register or use our platform:

Identity data: Full name, date of birth, gender, government-issued ID (National ID Card, Driver's Licence, Voter's Card, or Passport)
Contact data: Phone number, email address, residential address
Biometric data: Live selfie photograph (used only for manual face comparison against your ID document)
Financial data: Bank account details for receiving payments (property owners only); transaction reference numbers
Property data: Property address, title documents, geo-tagged photographs
Next of kin data: Name, phone number, relationship
Usage data: Pages visited, search queries, device type (collected anonymously)

We do not collect or store raw National Identification Numbers (NIN). Identity verification is conducted manually by our team using uploaded documents.

3. Why We Collect Your Data

We collect and process your personal data for the following lawful purposes:

✅ Identity verification — to confirm you are who you say you are before allowing property listings or transactions
✅ Fraud prevention — to detect and prevent fraudulent listings, impersonation, and illegal fee demands
✅ Transaction processing — to facilitate payments, generate agreements, and coordinate handovers
✅ Legal compliance — to comply with Nigerian law, including the Money Laundering (Prevention and Prohibition) Act 2022 and the NDPA 2023
✅ Law enforcement cooperation — to provide verified identity records to the Nigeria Police Force CID when a fraud complaint is filed, as disclosed in our consent agreement
✅ Platform communications — to send you verification updates, payment confirmations, and transaction alerts

4. How Long We Keep Your Data

We retain your personal data for as long as your account is active. If you close your account, we retain your data for 7 years in compliance with Nigerian financial record-keeping requirements. Biometric selfie data (face photographs) is retained for 2 years from the date of submission, after which it is deleted. Transaction records are retained for 7 years.

5. Who We Share Your Data With

Other verified platform users: Your verified badge and first name are visible to other verified users when you initiate a transaction. Your full contact details are only shared after both parties confirm interest.
GeoEstate lawyer panel: NBA-registered lawyers assigned to your transaction receive your verified identity and property documents as required to process the transaction.
Nigeria Police Force CID: In the event of a fraud complaint or dispute, your verified identity records and transaction trail are shared with the NPF Criminal Investigation Department. You consent to this when signing the platform consent agreement.
Payment processors: When Paystack is integrated (Phase 2), your bank account details are shared with Paystack solely for the purpose of processing transactions.

We do not sell your personal data to third parties. We do not share your data with advertisers.

6. Your Rights Under the NDPA 2023

As a data subject under the Nigeria Data Protection Act 2023, you have the following rights:

📋 Right to access — request a copy of all personal data we hold about you
✏️ Right to rectification — request correction of inaccurate data
🗑️ Right to erasure — request deletion of your data (subject to legal retention requirements)
🚫 Right to object — object to processing of your data for specific purposes
📦 Right to portability — receive your data in a structured, machine-readable format
⏸️ Right to restriction — request that we limit processing of your data in certain circumstances

To exercise any of these rights, email popson0205@gmail.com with the subject line "Data Subject Request." We will respond within 30 days.

7. Data Security

We implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to protect your personal data against unauthorised access, disclosure, alteration, or destruction. All data is transmitted over HTTPS. Document uploads are stored securely. Access to personal data is restricted to authorised GeoEstate staff on a need-to-know basis.

8. Cookies

GeoEstate uses only functional cookies — specifically localStorage — to remember your theme preference (light/dark mode) and session state. We do not use advertising cookies or third-party tracking cookies.

9. Changes to This Policy

We may update this Privacy Policy as our platform evolves or as Nigerian law changes. We will notify registered users of material changes by email or SMS at least 14 days before the change takes effect. The current version is always available at this page.

10. Complaints

If you believe we have handled your personal data in a manner that violates the NDPA 2023, you may file a complaint with the Nigeria Data Protection Commission (NDPC) at ndpc.gov.ng. We encourage you to contact us first at popson0205@gmail.com so we can resolve the matter directly.

Legal

Terms of Service

Effective date: 7 June 2026 · Popson Geospatial Services · These terms govern your use of the GeoEstate platform.

1. Acceptance of Terms

By registering on or using the GeoEstate platform (the "Platform"), you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service. If you do not agree, do not use the Platform. These terms apply to all users — property owners, renters, buyers, and lessees.

2. The Platform

GeoEstate is a technology platform that facilitates connections between verified property owners and verified renters/buyers in Nigeria. GeoEstate is not a real estate agent, broker, or property manager. We do not own any property listed on the Platform. We provide the technology, verification infrastructure, and transaction coordination — the contract is between the owner and the renter/buyer.

3. User Eligibility

You must be at least 18 years old to use the Platform. You must provide accurate, complete, and truthful information during registration and verification. You must be a Nigerian resident or have a valid Nigerian address to list or transact on the Platform.

4. Identity Verification

All users must complete identity verification before listing a property or initiating a transaction. Verification requires submission of a government-issued photo ID, a live selfie, and a next-of-kin declaration. Submitting false, forged, or misleading documents is a criminal offence under Nigerian law and will result in immediate account suspension, reporting to the Nigeria Police Force CID, and potential prosecution.

5. Property Listings

Property owners warrant that: (a) they are the lawful owner of the listed property or have legal authority to list it; (b) all submitted title documents are genuine and unencumbered; (c) all property information, photographs, and pricing are accurate. GeoEstate reserves the right to remove any listing that fails verification or is found to be misleading, fraudulent, or in breach of these terms.

6. Platform Fee

GeoEstate charges a 10% platform fee on every successfully completed transaction. This fee is deducted from the total transaction amount before the property owner receives their 90% share. The platform fee is non-negotiable and non-refundable once a transaction is completed. The fee covers verification, transaction coordination, document management, lawyer panel access, and NPF liaison services.

7. Prohibited Conduct

The following are strictly prohibited on the Platform:

❌ Requesting, demanding, or accepting any payment outside the GeoEstate platform
❌ Charging any fee not disclosed in the platform's fee structure
❌ Listing a property you do not own or have authority to list
❌ Submitting false or forged identity or property documents
❌ Impersonating another person or a GeoEstate staff member
❌ Using the Platform to facilitate money laundering or any illegal activity
❌ Harassing, threatening, or intimidating any other user
❌ Circumventing the verification process by any means

Violation of any prohibition may result in immediate account suspension, forfeiture of any funds in escrow, reporting to the Nigeria Police Force CID, and civil or criminal legal action.

8. Payments & Refunds

All payments must be made through the GeoEstate platform. During Phase 1, payments are made by bank transfer to the GeoEstate designated account. The account details will be provided by the GeoEstate sales team before any transaction is finalised — do not transfer to any account unless confirmed by GeoEstate staff. A unique reference code is required in the transfer narration. Funds are released to property owners only after handover is confirmed by both parties on the Platform. GeoEstate's dedicated CBN-compliant escrow account is currently being processed and will be integrated in Phase 2 (Paystack integration). If a listing is found to be fraudulent after payment but before handover, the full amount is refunded to the renter/buyer within 5 business days. Disputes are handled per Section 10.

9. Lawyer Panel

GeoEstate maintains a panel of NBA-registered lawyers who are assigned to high-value transactions (buy transactions and commercial leases above ₦5,000,000). Lawyer fees are charged separately from the platform fee and are disclosed before assignment. GeoEstate is not a law firm and does not provide legal advice. Lawyers on the panel are independent professionals.

10. Dispute Resolution

If a dispute arises between a property owner and a renter/buyer, either party may file a complaint through the GeoEstate dispute form. GeoEstate will review the complaint within 2 hours and attempt to mediate. If fraud is confirmed, GeoEstate will submit a case file to the NPF CID containing the verified identities of both parties and the full transaction trail. For civil disputes, GeoEstate may refer the matter to a panel lawyer for arbitration. GeoEstate's decision on dispute resolution is final for the purposes of escrow fund release.

11. Limitation of Liability

GeoEstate is a technology platform and is not liable for: (a) the condition or quality of any property listed; (b) the conduct of any user after identity verification; (c) losses arising from transactions conducted outside the Platform; (d) delays in verification or fund release caused by circumstances beyond our control. GeoEstate's total liability to any user for any claim arising from use of the Platform shall not exceed the platform fee paid in respect of the relevant transaction.

12. Governing Law

These Terms of Service are governed by the laws of the Federal Republic of Nigeria. Any dispute arising from these terms that cannot be resolved through the Platform's dispute resolution process shall be subject to the jurisdiction of the courts of Lagos State, Nigeria.

13. Changes to These Terms

GeoEstate may update these Terms of Service from time to time. We will notify registered users of material changes at least 14 days before they take effect. Continued use of the Platform after the effective date constitutes acceptance of the updated terms.

14. Contact

For questions about these Terms: popson0205@gmail.com · +234 916 042 0100 · WhatsApp: +234 916 042 0100

How to Process a Payment
Step-by-step for your team

Property Management
Edit, reprice, or remove any property at any time. Completed transactions auto-archive.
PropertyOwnerTypePriceStatusListedActions
Tenancy Tracker
Track all active rent and lease agreements. Automatic reminders at 2 months, 2 weeks, and expiry.
ReferencePropertyTenantOwnerType StartEnd DateDays LeftStatusActions
📋 Renewal & Packing-Out Policy
2 months before expiry: Tenant notified to confirm renewal or give notice to vacate.
2 weeks before expiry: Final reminder. If no renewal payment, packing-out process begins.
On expiry: Tenant given 3 weeks to vacate the property.
Renewal: Tenant makes payment via GeoEstate platform. New agreement generated automatically.
⚖️ Owner Termination Policy
If a property owner wishes to terminate an active tenancy before expiry:
1. A GeoEstate panel lawyer conducts an evaluation of the remaining term.
2. The owner bears all costs — including tenant relocation allowance and any penalty fees.
3. A minimum 3-month notice must be given to the tenant.
4. Escrow funds for the remaining period are refunded to the tenant in full.
Team Management
GeoEstate staff and their roles
Live Sync Active — Admin actions (approve/reject/go-live) are written to shared localStorage. The main website reads this data on load and reflects the current verified state.

Sync Log
Recent admin → website pushes
How Sync Works
Technical overview for the development team

Both the admin dashboard and the main website share a localStorage key (pgs_sync) when opened in the same browser. In production, this will be replaced by a REST API — the admin writes to the database, the website reads from it.

Current sync events: Property approved → goes live on website · Property rejected → removed from website · User suspended → listings hidden · Dispute resolved → case closed

Production integration path: POST /api/admin/approve-property → webhook triggers website cache invalidation → listing appears within 30 seconds.
